remote_work.name: The Work Loft Silom
remote_work.short_note: Clean co-working in Bangkok's business district. Consistent air-con, real desk space, and WiFi that holds a call.
remote_work.why_good: Central Silom location means easy BTS access. Handles calls without the café noise problem.
remote_work.watch_out: Peak hours (10am–noon) can fill up. Some days require booking ahead.

remote_work.name: Launchpad
remote_work.short_note: One of Bangkok's established co-working spaces with a genuine community feel. Popular with long-stay nomads.
remote_work.why_good: Reliable infrastructure. Hot desks and private pods available. Good for month-long stays.
remote_work.watch_out: Can get busy during startup events. Check the schedule before dropping in.

remote_work.name: The Hive Thonglor
remote_work.short_note: Design-forward co-working in trendy Thonglor. Comfortable and professional for longer work sessions.
remote_work.why_good: Strong WiFi, ergonomic seating, and a quiet atmosphere that actually holds focus.
remote_work.watch_out: Thonglor is pricier. Day pass rates add up over a full week.

cafes_bakery.name: Sarnies Bangkok
cafes_bakery.short_note: Singapore import that nails the specialty coffee and good-food formula. Reliably good flat whites.
cafes_bakery.why_good: Consistent quality where the food actually matches the coffee. Good table-to-outlet ratio.
cafes_bakery.watch_out: Popular spot — arrive before 9am or after 2pm to avoid queues.

cafes_bakery.name: Artis Coffee
cafes_bakery.short_note: Serious about coffee without the attitude. Clean space, quality beans, and a calm atmosphere for morning work sessions.
cafes_bakery.why_good: Multiple roasters on rotation. Real barista knowledge. Good table-to-outlet ratio.
cafes_bakery.watch_out: Small space. Not suited for marathon 5-hour sessions.

cafes_bakery.name: Holey Artisan Bakery
cafes_bakery.short_note: Bangkok's best bagels alongside excellent sourdough. Feels like a real bakery, not just Instagram content.
cafes_bakery.why_good: Consistently well-executed bakes. Their coffee programme is solid. Great for a working breakfast.
cafes_bakery.watch_out: Pastries sell out by mid-morning on weekends.

local_food.name: Kajohn
local_food.short_note: Proper Thai street-food restaurant that's been around for decades. No tourist menu, no English upsell.
local_food.why_good: Pad see ew and khao man gai at prices that make you wonder why you'd eat anywhere else.
local_food.watch_out: Cash only. Very busy at lunch. Basic seating — eat fast or go off-peak.

local_food.name: Kurissara Thai Cuisine
local_food.short_note: Thoughtfully curated Thai menu without tourist-trap pricing. Worth it for a proper sit-down Thai meal.
local_food.why_good: Quality ingredients, attentive service, and dishes that taste like actual home cooking.
local_food.watch_out: Pricier than street food, but the gap is justified. Reservations help on weekends.

practical.name: GO Laundry PX
practical.short_note: Reliable drop-off laundry service. Clean results, no drama, next-day turnaround.
practical.why_good: Straightforward pricing and a central location near BTS Phrom Phong. Professional handling.
practical.watch_out: Same-day service costs extra. Drop off before noon for next-day pickup.

practical.name: Laundry Service Bangkok
practical.short_note: Solid backup laundry option. Multiple locations, per-kilo pricing, handles bulk loads without fuss.
practical.why_good: Affordable and accessible. Good for weeks-long stays where laundry becomes a logistics task.
practical.watch_out: Quality can vary between branches. Check recent reviews for your specific location.

explore.name: Mahanakhon Skywalk
explore.short_note: Glass-floor observation deck at the top of Bangkok's tallest building. Worth doing once.
explore.why_good: Unobstructed 360° views of Bangkok. The glass floor section is genuinely impressive at sunset.
explore.watch_out: Pricey entry (~฿875). Go at sunset for the best light. Skip entirely on cloudy days.

explore.name: Wat Arun Viewing Point
explore.short_note: The Wat Arun riverside view at golden hour is one of Bangkok's iconic sights — and free from the opposite bank.
explore.why_good: The view from the Tha Tien pier side is better than being inside the temple itself.
explore.watch_out: Very busy on weekends. Go early morning for clean photos and breathing room.
